summary_ja: フィリピン南方沖で発生したマグニチュード7.8の地震を受け、月曜日に沖縄など日本各地で津波が観測された。気象庁は太平洋沿岸に津波注意報を発表したが、午後には解除された。フィリピンでは少なくとも19人が死亡し、日本では10都道府県で一時19万人以上に避難が呼びかけられた。

sentences.grammar_ja: 本文ではなく、Google Tag Manager用のHTML埋め込みコード。ニュースサイトの計測タグで、英語学習の文法対象ではない。

sentences.text: Rescuers inspect the damage after an earthquake in General Santos, Philippines, on Monday.Image:Philippine Red Cross via AP
sentences.grammar_ja: 写真キャプション。inspect the damage（被害を調査する）のコロケーション。after an earthquakeは時系列。末尾のImage:は出典表記で、通常はスペースを入れる（on Monday. Image:）。

sentences.text: Tsunami waves arrive in Japan after M7.8 quake strikes off Philippines
sentences.grammar_ja: 見出し文体。現在形で「今起きている」臨場感を出すニュース慣用。M7.8 quakeはmagnitude 7.8 earthquakeの省略。strikes off Philippinesは「フィリピン沖で発生」。

sentences.text: Tsunami waves arrived in Okinawa and other parts of Japan on Monday after an earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.8 struck off the southern Philippines, according to Japanese and U.S. authorities.
sentences.grammar_ja: after節＋過去形の時系列（地震→津波）。with a preliminary magnitude of 7.8は地震の規模を修飾。according to ... authoritiesは報道の出典を示す定型表現。

sentences.text: The Japan Meteorological Agency said at a press conference that a minor tsunami was observed on the main island of Okinawa at around 12:18 p.m. after the agency issued a tsunami advisory for the Pacific coast along the eastern, western and southern parts of the country.
sentences.grammar_ja: said that節で間接話法。was observedは受動態（観測された）。after the agency issued ...は「注意報発表のあと」という順序。along the ... coastは沿岸を表す前置詞。

sentences.text: Also, a 20-centimeter tsunami was observed at Chichijima Island of the Ogasawara Islands in the Pacific, about 1,000 kilometers south of central Tokyo, around 1:46 p.m.
sentences.grammar_ja: Alsoで追加情報。20-centimeterはハイフンで単位＋名詞をつなぐ。about 1,000 kilometers south of ...は距離＋方位の説明。at ... Island / around ... p.m.は場所・時刻。

sentences.text: The agency alerted the public earlier in the day that tsunami waves of up to 1 meter could reach Miyako Island and the Yaeyama islets in Okinawa and elsewhere.
sentences.grammar_ja: alerted ... that節。earlier in the dayは「その日のうちに早い時間帯」。up to 1 meterは「最大1メートルまで」。could reachは可能性・予測。

sentences.text: The advisory, which covered areas stretching from Ibaraki Prefecture near Tokyo to Japan's southernmost island prefecture of Okinawa, was lifted in the afternoon.
sentences.grammar_ja: 関係代名詞whichの非制限用法。stretching from A to Bは範囲を示す分詞構文。was liftedは受動態（解除された）。

sentences.text: The quake struck 26 km southwest of Kablalan in the Philippines at a depth of 55.2 km at around 7:37 a.m. local time, according to the U.S.
sentences.grammar_ja: 地震の位置・深さ・時刻を並列。southwest of / at a depth of / at around ... local timeは災害報道の定型。文末のthe U.S.は次文とつながる省略形。

sentences.text: Philippine authorities said at least 19 people died and more than 130 were injured.
sentences.grammar_ja: at leastは「少なくとも」。died（能動）とwere injured（受動）の対比。more than 130は injuredの前で人数を修飾。

sentences.text: In December 2023, an M7.6 quake hit the area, according to the USGS.
sentences.grammar_ja: 過去の出来事を背景として提示。hit the areaは「その地域を襲った」。according to the USGSは出典表記。

sentences.text: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said on social media she has instructed relevant ministries and agencies to take measures to prevent damage.
sentences.grammar_ja: saidの後にthat省略の間接話法。has instructed ... to take measuresは「〜するよう指示した」。relevant ministries and agenciesは行政機関。to prevent damageは目的。

sentences.text: Over 190,000 people in 10 prefectures, including Chiba, Kochi and Okinawa, were urged to evacuate at one point, according to the Fire and Disaster Management Agency.
sentences.grammar_ja: were urged to evacuateは受動態＋不定詞（避難を呼びかけられた）。including ...は例示。at one pointは「一時的に」。Over 190,000は概数。

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 津波注意報（大きな津波の可能性を伝える警報）
vocabulary.example: The agency issued a tsunami advisory for the Pacific coast.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 暫定マグニチュード（速報値の震度規模）
vocabulary.example: an earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.8
an M7.8 quake strikes off the southern Philippines
The advisory was lifted in the afternoon.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 避難を強く呼びかける
vocabulary.example: Over 190,000 people were urged to evacuate.

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 一時的に、ある時点では
vocabulary.example: were urged to evacuate at one point
Rescuers inspect the damage after an earthquake.
instructed ministries to take measures to prevent damage

grammar_points.title: Headline present tense
grammar_points.rule: News headlines often use the simple present tense to make events feel immediate.
grammar_points.example: Tsunami waves arrive in Japan after M7.8 quake strikes off Philippines
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 見出しでは過去形ではなく現在形を使い、出来事を「今まさに起きている」ように見せる。

grammar_points.title: According to + source
grammar_points.rule: Use 'according to' to attribute information to an official source or organization.
grammar_points.example: according to Japanese and U.S. authorities
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 情報の出所を明示する報道の定型表現。「〜によると」で権威ある情報源に帰属させる。

grammar_points.title: Passive voice in disaster news
grammar_points.rule: Passive forms (was observed, were injured, were urged) focus on what happened rather than who did it.
grammar_points.example: a minor tsunami was observed on the main island of Okinawa
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 災害報道では受動態が多く、観測・被害・呼びかけなど事実そのものに焦点を当てる。

grammar_points.title: Time sequence with after
grammar_points.rule: Use 'after' + past event to show one action happened following another.
grammar_points.example: Tsunami waves arrived ... after an earthquake ... struck
grammar_points.meaning_ja: after節で原因・先行事件とその結果の時間順序を示す。地震→津波のような因果の流れを作る。
